title: Cache Simulator
description: A low-level cache memory simulator supporting LRU, LFU, and FIFO eviction policies with step-by-step or fully automatic address sequence processing, visual cache slot grid with hit/miss/eviction animations, and a real-time hit-ratio line graph rendered natively on Canvas.
author: Girish Madarkar
features: 3 eviction algorithms: LRU (scans for min lastAccess tick, O(n) per evict), LFU (min freq with lastAccess tiebreak, O(n) scan), FIFO (min insertOrder, O(n) scan) — each in dedicated function

Cache slot grid: up to 12 visual slots rendered as DOM cards showing block address, frequency counter, and last-access tick metadata. Slot .hit class triggers scale(1.1) keyframe + green glow (#00ff66 box-shadow), .miss class triggers pink background flash via flashMiss keyframe, .evict class applies crimson glow

Address sequence input: comma-separated integers parsed via split(',').map(parseInt). Step button processes one address at a time; Run All button asynchronously loops through remaining addresses with speed-gated delay (ms = max(50, 300 − speed×12)) using setTimeout Promise wrapper

Hit-ratio graph: 80-sample rolling window appended on each processAddress() call. Canvas renderer draws filled area (rgba(0,255,102,0.04)) + stroked line (#00ff66, width 1.5) with 5 horizontal grid lines at 20% intervals

Cache miss + eviction pipeline: on miss, if cache.length >= capacity, findEvict*() selects victim → removes via splice → flashes evicted slot → inserts new block with freq=1, insertOrder=++tick. Last evicted address shown in telemetry
Telemetry panel: total lookups, hits, misses, hit ratio percentage (hits/(hits+misses)×100), last evicted block address
Purge & Reset: zeroes all counters, empties cache array, clears ratio history, re-renders empty grid and blank graph without page reload
